summary: The review of "Subcontract Example 2.pdf" indicates a generally low-risk posture, with most clauses falling outside the scope of the University of Auckland's Preferred Contracting Positions and flagged as informational (blue). However, two critical issues were identified: the confidentiality clause does not align with UoA Position #POS-11, requiring exclusions for public or independently developed information and a five-year confidentiality limit, and the warranty clause introduces obligations beyond UoA Position #POS-10, necessitating escalation. Additionally, the intellectual property clause must be referred to Auckland UniServices Ltd as per UoA Position #POS-IP. The next step is to escalate the flagged clauses to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts, for review and negotiation.

flags.clause_title: “Confidential Information” means all non-public, proprietary, or confidential
flags.snippet: “Confidential Information” means all non-public, proprietary, or confidential information disclosed by one party to the other, whether orally, in writing, or in any other form, including but not limited to technical data, trade secrets, know-how, research, product plans, products, services, customers, customer lists, markets, software, developments, inventions, processes, formulas, technology, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware configuration information, marketing, finances, or other business information;
flags.rationale: The clause does not align with UoA Position #POS-11 on Confidentiality. It fails to exclude information already public, independently developed, previously known by the receiving party, or legally required to be disclosed. Additionally, it does not specify UoA's ability to disclose to agents and Affiliates, nor does it limit the duration of confidentiality obligations to 5 years post-completion. These omissions materially deviate from the Preferred tier and require escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for review and amendment.
flags.text:
“Confidential Information” means all non-public, proprietary, or confidential information disclosed by one party to the other, whether orally, in writing, or in any other form, including but not limited to technical data, trade secrets, know-how, research, product

plans, products, services, customers, customer lists, markets, software, developments,
inventions, processes, formulas, technology, designs, drawings, engineering, hardware
configuration information, marketing, finances, or other business information;

flags.clause_title: The Developer warrants that all work performed under this Agreement will be:
flags.snippet: The Developer warrants that all work performed under this Agreement will be: - Performed in a professional and workmanlike manner; - Completed in accordance with the specifications and requirements set forth herein; - Free from defects in materials and workmanship; - Compliant with all applicable laws, regulations, and industry standards.
flags.rationale: The clause introduces warranties beyond the UoA Preferred Contracting Position (POS-10), which explicitly limits warranties to (a) authority to enter into the contract and (b) performance to a professional standard/usual good practice. The additional warranties regarding 'free from defects in materials and workmanship' and 'compliance with all applicable laws, regulations, and industry standards' materially deviate from the Preferred Position and are not pre-approved under the Acceptable tier. This requires escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for review and potential negotiation.
flags.text:
The Developer warrants that all work performed under this Agreement will be: -
Performed in a professional and workmanlike manner; - Completed in accordance with the
specifications and requirements set forth herein; - Free from defects in materials and
workmanship; - Compliant with all applicable laws, regulations, and industry standards.

flags.clause_title: “Intellectual Property” means all intellectual property rights of any kind,
flags.snippet: “Intellectual Property” means all intellectual property rights of any kind, including but not limited to patents, patent applications, trademarks, trademark applications, service marks, trade names, copyrights, trade secrets, know-how, inventions, processes, formulas, source and object codes, data, programs, other works of authorship, discoveries, improvements, and other proprietary rights;
flags.rationale: The clause pertains to Intellectual Property, which is explicitly excluded from UoA Contracting Positions and must be referred to Auckland UniServices Ltd for review. This aligns with UoA Position #POS-IP, which mandates referral of all IP clauses regardless of wording.
flags.text:
“Intellectual Property” means all intellectual property rights of any kind, including but
not limited to patents, patent applications, trademarks, trademark applications, service
marks, trade names, copyrights, trade secrets, know-how, inventions, processes, formulas,
source and object codes, data, programs, other works of authorship, discoveries,
improvements, and other proprietary rights;

flags.clause_title: In this Agreement: - References to clauses, schedules, and appendices are
flags.snippet: In this Agreement: - References to clauses, schedules, and appendices are references to clauses, schedules, and appendices of this Agreement; - Headings are for convenience only and do not affect interpretation; - Words importing the singular include the plural and vice versa; - References to any statute or statutory provision include any subordinate legislation made under it and any modification, extension, or re-enactment thereof; - The word 'including' shall mean 'including without limitation.'
flags.rationale: This clause deals with general interpretation rules for the agreement, such as references, headings, singular/plural usage, statutory references, and the meaning of 'including.' It does not touch on the specific topic of Variations and Extensions (UoA Position #POS-21) or any other UoA Preferred Contracting Position. The clause is unrelated to any flagged policy area and falls outside the scope of the Positions document.
flags.standard_ref: Not covered by UoA Positions or Template
flags.text:
In this Agreement: - References to clauses, schedules, and appendices are references to
clauses, schedules, and appendices of this Agreement; - Headings are for convenience only
and do not affect interpretation; - Words importing the singular include the plural and vice
versa; - References to any statute or statutory provision include any subordinate legislation
made under it and any modification, extension, or re-enactment thereof; - The word
“including” shall mean “including without limitation.”

flags.clause_title: The Developer shall provide the Client with regular progress reports as
flags.snippet: The Developer shall provide the Client with regular progress reports as specified in Schedule A (Work Plan), including but not limited to: - Monthly written progress reports detailing work completed, milestones achieved, and upcoming activities; - Immediate notification of any material changes to project scope, timeline, or deliverables; - Access to work-in-progress materials and documentation as reasonably requested by the Client.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses reporting obligations, which is not covered by any UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template for this contract type. As such, it falls outside the scope of the documented policy and is flagged as blue.

The Developer shall provide the Client with regular progress reports as specified in
Schedule A (Work Plan), including but not limited to: - Monthly written progress reports
detailing work completed, milestones achieved, and upcoming activities; - Immediate
notification of any material changes to project scope, timeline, or deliverables; - Access to
work-in-progress materials and documentation as reasonably requested by the Client.

flags.clause_title: The Developer will allocate the named individuals identified in Schedule A
flags.snippet: The Developer will allocate the named individuals identified in Schedule A (Work Plan) to perform the work. The Developer will inform the Client of any changes to the availability of Key Personnel, who will have the right to review and approve any changes.
flags.rationale: This clause addresses personnel allocation and changes to Key Personnel availability, which is not covered by any UoA Preferred Contracting Position or template. It does not directly relate to Governing Law and Jurisdiction as flagged by the comparator.
flags.standard_ref: No applicable UoA Position or template
flags.text:
The Developer will allocate the named individuals identified in Schedule A (Work Plan) to
perform the work. The Developer will inform the Client of any changes to the availability of
Key Personnel, who will have the right to review and approve any changes.
4. LIABILITY
4.1 Each party:
- acknowledges that the Services are being supplied and acquired in trade;
- agrees that the provisions of the Consumer Guarantees Act 1993 and sections 9, 12A
and 13 of the Fair Trading Act will not apply to this contract; and
-
agrees that it is fair and reasonable that it is bound by the provisions of this clause
4.1
.
4.2 Except where expressly stated otherwise, disclaims all warranties, either express
or implied, including, without limitation, warranties of merchantability and fitness for a
particular purpose.
4.3 Neither party, or their employees, agents or contractors, will be liable to the other for any
actions, damages, claims, liabilities, costs, expenses, or losses in any way arising in
relation to this contract that exceed an amount equal to the Fee paid by the Client to
under this contract.
4.4 In no event will either party, their employees, agents or contractors be liable for
consequential, special, indirect, incidental, punitive or exemplary damages, costs,
expenses or losses (including, without limitation, lost profits and opportunity costs).
4.5 Clauses 4.3 and 4.4 apply regardless of the form of action, damage, claim, liability, cost,
expense or loss, whether in contract, statute, tort (including, without limitation,
negligence) or otherwise. None of the exclusions or limitations will have the effect of
limiting or excluding any form of liability where such liability cannot be so limited or
excluded under applicable law.
4.6 The Client understands and agrees that the Services provided may include advice and
recommendations, but all decisions in connection with the implementation of such
advice and recommendations will be the responsibility of, and made by, the Client. The
Client will hold harmless against any claim for costs or damages arising from that
implementation.
4.7 indemnifies the Client, and its personnel against all loss, damage, cost and expense
arising from third party claims, and against fines, penalties and other sanctions imposed by
any government or regulatory authority, in relation to performance of the Services.

4.8 Clause 4.8 does not apply to the extent that any loss, damage, cost or expense arises as a
result of any indemnified person’s negligence, breach of law or breach of this Agreement
(including any breach of warranty).
5. PAYMENT TERMS
5.1 Payment Schedule
Payment for the Project shall be made in accordance with Schedule B (Payment Schedule)
attached hereto and incorporated by reference. All payments are subject to the following
terms:
a) Milestone-Based Payments: Payments shall be made upon successful completion and
acceptance of specified milestones as detailed in Schedule B;
b) GST: All amounts specified in this Agreement are exclusive of Goods and Services Tax
(GST). GST shall be added to all invoices at the applicable rate;
c) Payment Terms: Unless otherwise specified, all invoices shall be paid on 20th of the
month following receipt of invoice by the Client;
d) Final Payment: Final payment shall be made upon successful delivery and acceptance
of all deliverables and completion of the Project to the Client’s reasonable satisfaction.
5.2 Invoicing Requirements
The Developer shall submit invoices that include: - Detailed description of work completed
and milestones achieved; - Reference to the applicable milestone or deliverable from
Schedule B; - GST registration number and appropriate GST calculations; - Supporting
documentation as reasonably required by the Client.
5.3 Payment Disputes
In the event of a payment dispute, the Client shall pay any undisputed portion of an invoice
within the specified payment terms and notify the Developer in writing of the specific basis
for any disputed amounts within seven (7) days of receipt of the invoice.
6. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY
6.1 Ownership of Developed IP
Subject to full payment of all fees and charges due under this Agreement, all Intellectual
Property developed, created, conceived, or reduced to practice by the Developer in the

course of performing the Project, including but not limited to inventions, discoveries,
improvements, designs, specifications, software, documentation, and know-how
(collectively, the “Developed IP”), shall be and remain the sole and exclusive property of
the Client.
6.2 Assignment of Rights
The Developer hereby assigns, transfers, and conveys to the Client all right, title, and
interest in and to the Developed IP, including all patent rights, copyrights, trade secret
rights, and other proprietary rights therein. This assignment shall be effective immediately
upon creation of such Developed IP, subject to the condition precedent of full payment as
specified in Section 5.1.
6.3 Pre-Existing IP
Each party shall retain ownership of any Intellectual Property that existed prior to the
commencement of this Agreement (“Pre-Existing IP”). The Developer hereby grants to the
Client a non-exclusive, royalty-free, perpetual license to use any of the Developer’s Pre-
Existing IP that is incorporated into or necessary for the use of the Developed IP, solely for
the purposes contemplated by this Agreement.
6.4 Third-Party IP
The Developer warrants that, based on its best intent and knowledge at the time of
performance, the Developed IP will not knowingly infringe upon any third-party
Intellectual Property rights. The Developer represents that it will conduct reasonable due
diligence to avoid infringement of known third-party Intellectual Property rights during
the development process. However, the Developer does not provide any indemnification or
guarantee against potential third-party intellectual property claims that may arise from the
use of the Developed IP.
6.5 Moral Rights
To the extent permitted by law, the Developer waives all moral rights in the Developed IP
in favour of the Client.
6.6 Further Assurances
The Developer agrees to execute such additional documents and take such additional
actions as may be reasonably requested by the Client to evidence, perfect, or enforce the
Client’s ownership rights in the Developed IP.
6.7 Disclosure of Inventions
The Developer shall promptly disclose to the Client in writing all inventions, discoveries,
improvements, and other intellectual property created or conceived during the
performance of this Agreement, whether or not such intellectual property is patentable or
subject to copyright protection.

6.8 Patent Applications
If the Client elects to file patent applications for any Developed IP, the Developer agrees to
cooperate fully with such patent application process, including providing necessary
documentation, executing required forms, and making inventors available for examination
or testimony as reasonably required.
7. CONFIDENTIALITY
7.1 Confidentiality Obligations
Each party acknowledges that it may have access to Confidential Information of the other
party. Each party agrees to:
a) Hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence;
b) Not disclose Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written
consent of the disclosing party;
c) Use Confidential Information solely for the purposes of this Agreement;
d) Take reasonable precautions to protect the confidentiality of such information, using
at least the same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential information,
but in no event less than reasonable care.
7.2 Exceptions
The obligations set forth in Section 7.1 shall not apply to information that: a) Is or becomes
publicly available through no breach of this Agreement; b) Was rightfully known by the
receiving party prior to disclosure; c) Is rightfully received by the receiving party from a
third party without breach of any confidentiality obligation; d) Is independently developed
by the receiving party without use of or reference to the Confidential Information; e) Is
required to be disclosed by law or court order, provided that the receiving party gives the
disclosing party reasonable advance notice of such required disclosure.
7.3 Return of Information
Upon termination of this Agreement or upon request by the disclosing party, each party
shall promptly return or destroy all documents, materials, and other tangible
manifestations of Confidential Information in its possession or control.
7.4 Survival
The obligations under this Section 6 shall survive termination of this Agreement for a
period of five (5) years.

8. DISPUTE RESOLUTION
8.1 Negotiation
In the event of any dispute, controversy, or claim arising out of or relating to this
Agreement or the breach, termination, or invalidity thereof (a “Dispute”), the parties shall
first attempt to resolve such Dispute through good faith negotiations between senior
representatives of each party who have authority to settle the Dispute.
8.2 Mediation
If the parties are unable to resolve the Dispute through negotiation within thirty (30) days
after written notice of the Dispute, the parties agree to submit the
Mediation
Rules then in effect. The mediation shall be conducted in Auckland, New Zealand, unless the
parties agree otherwise in writing.
8.3 Costs
Each party shall bear its own costs and expenses in connection with any dispute resolution
proceedings, except that the costs of mediation (including the mediator’s fees) shall be
borne equally by the parties.
9. TERMINATION
9.1 Termination for Convenience
Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time upon thirty (30) days’ prior written
notice to the other party.
9.2 Termination for Cause
Either party may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice if: a) The
other party materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within thirty
(30) days after written notice thereof; b) The other party becomes insolvent, makes an
assignment for the benefit of creditors, files a petition in bankruptcy, or has a petition in
bankruptcy filed against it; c) The other party ceases to carry on business in the ordinary
course.
9.3 Effect of Termination
Upon termination of this Agreement:
a) Payment for Completed Work: The Client shall pay the Developer for all work
satisfactorily completed prior to the effective date of termination on a pro-rata basis
according to the milestones achieved and deliverables accepted;

b) Return of Materials: Each party shall promptly return to the other party all
Confidential Information, documents, materials, and property belonging to such other
party;
c) Survival of Obligations: Termination shall not affect any rights or obligations that
have accrued prior to termination, and the provisions of Sections 6 (Intellectual
Property), 7 (Confidentiality), 8 (Dispute Resolution), and 10 (General Provisions)
shall survive termination.
9.4 No Penalty for Termination
Neither party shall be liable to the other for any damages solely as a result of terminating
this Agreement in accordance with its terms.
10. GENERAL PROVISIONS
10.1 Governing Law
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of New
Zealand, without regard to its conflict of laws principles.
10.2 Jurisdiction
The parties hereby submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of New Zealand for the
resolution of any disputes arising under this Agreement, except as otherwise provided in
Section 7 (Dispute Resolution).
10.3 Entire Agreement
This Agreement, including all schedules and appendices attached hereto, constitutes the
entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and
supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, understandings, negotiations, and
discussions, whether oral or written, between the parties relating to such subject matter.
10.4 Amendment
This Agreement may be amended only by a written instrument signed by both parties.
10.5 Severability
If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable, the
validity, legality, and enforceability of the remaining provisions shall not be affected or
impaired thereby.
10.6 Waiver
No waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be deemed or shall constitute a waiver
of any other provision. No waiver shall be effective unless it is in writing and signed by the
party to be charged with such waiver.

10.7 Assignment
Neither party may assign this Agreement or any rights or obligations hereunder without
the prior written consent of the other party, except that the Client may assign this
Agreement to any affiliate or in connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of all or
substantially all of its assets.
10.8 Force Majeure
Neither party shall be liable for any failure or delay in performance under this Agreement
that is due to fire, flood, earthquake, elements of nature, acts of God, acts of war, terrorism,
riots, civil disorders, rebellions, or other causes beyond the reasonable control of such
party.
10.9 Health and Safety
undertakes to comply with the provisions of the Health and Safety at Work Act
2015 insofar as those provisions relate to the performance by of any obligations on
under this Agreement. does not assume any obligations as the Client’s
agent that may be imposed upon the Client pursuant to the or any other applicable
health and safety laws in relation to this contract.
10.10 Notices
All notices required or permitted under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be
deemed given when delivered personally, sent by confirmed facsimile transmission, sent by
confirmed electronic mail, or three (3) days after being sent by registered or certified mail,
return receipt requested, to the addresses set forth in the preamble to this Agreement or to
such other address as either party may specify in writing.
10.11 Counterparts
This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an
original and all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
11. EXECUTION
IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the date last
signed below.
University of Auckland
